title: Mesmerizing vortexes swirl near an Antarctic volcano | Space photo of the day for May 12, 2026
summary: A stunning space photo from May 12, 2026, captures mesmerizing vortexes swirling near an Antarctic volcano, created by arctic winds.

title: Star Catcher just raised $65 million to build the world's first power grid in space — with lasers
summary: Star Catcher Industries raised $65 million to develop a constellation of power-beaming satellites, aiming to create the world's first space-based power grid using lasers.

title: A hidden wildfire pollutant causes thousands of excess deaths per year, satellite data shows
summary: Satellite data reveals that wildfire-derived ozone causes approximately 2,045 excess deaths annually in the U.S. over the past 20 years.

title: SpaceX fuels up Starship V3 megarocket for 1st time ahead of crucial test flight (photos)
summary: SpaceX has successfully stacked and fueled the new Starship V3 megarocket for the first time, preparing for a test flight that may occur this week.

title: NASA's Artemis 3 rocket is taking shape for 2027 launch to test lunar landers (photo)
summary: NASA's Artemis 3 rocket core stage has been raised in the Vehicle Assembly Building, awaiting engine integration for its planned 2027 launch. The mission aims to test lunar landers as part of the Artemis program.

title: Satellite spies one of Russia's most active volcanoes melting snow from the inside out
summary: Fresh satellite images reveal volcanic heat melting snow around Russia's Shiveluch volcano on the Kamchatka Peninsula. The volcano is one of Russia's most active, showing signs of internal activity.

title: China's Tianzhou 10 freighter delivers 7 tons of cargo to Tiangong space station
summary: China's Tianzhou 10 freighter successfully delivered nearly seven tons of supplies to the Tiangong space station. The cargo mission launched atop a Long March 7 rocket and docked within five hours.

title: How NASA lunar scientists taught Artemis 2 astronauts to see the moon with different eyes
summary: NASA lunar scientists trained Artemis 2 astronauts to observe and describe the moon in a way that enhances scientific understanding. The astronauts' observations revealed unexpected details about the lunar environment.

title: SpaceX launches secret US spy satellites to orbit from California (video, photos)
summary: SpaceX launched a batch of secret U.S. spy satellites into orbit from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California. The mission took place under foggy conditions on May 11.

title: Australia’s Cloudy Beauty
summary: Australia's Victorian Alps experienced fog formation and an arch-shaped cloud over Port Phillip Bay. The weather phenomenon occurred without specific space industry relevance.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4886-4892: Ingenuity and Perseverance, Curiosity Style
summary: The Curiosity rover's recent activities on Mars were compared to the Ingenuity helicopter and Perseverance rover from the Mars 2020 mission. The blog highlights the rover's scientific achievements over sols 4886-4892.

title: NASA’s Curiosity Takes Close Look at Rock That Got Stuck on Drill
summary: NASA's Curiosity Mars rover captured an image of a rock named 'Atacama' that had become lodged in its drill on April 25, 2026. Engineers spent days addressing the issue.

title: May 2026 Satellite Puzzler
summary: The 'May 2026 Satellite Puzzler' challenges participants to identify the location of a satellite image and explain its significance. This event falls under a community-driven space science or outreach activity.

title: Nicholas Houghton: Engineering Crew Safety for NASA’s Artemis Missions
summary: Nicholas Houghton works as an engineer at NASA, designing safety systems for the Orion spacecraft, including the Artemis II mission. He joined NASA as an intern and now contributes to crew survival systems.

title: NASA Invites Media to Annual Lunabotics Robotics Competition
summary: NASA announced its 2026 Lunabotics Robotics Competition, inviting media to attend the event at the Kennedy Space Center Visitor Complex in Florida from May 19 to 21. The competition will feature live robotics challenges and run daily from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m.

title: Joint Earth Observation Mission Quality Assessment Framework – Optical Guidelines Documents Released
summary: A new Optical Guidelines document has been released to standardize the quality assessment process for optical data from commercial Earth Observation missions. This framework aims to ensure transparency and repeatability in evaluating mission data.

title: Hubble Survey Sets Up Roman’s Future Look Near Milky Way’s Center
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope has conducted a survey of the Milky Way's galactic bulge to prepare for future observations by the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope. This dense region of stars and planets will be further studied by the upcoming Roman Space Telescope.

title: NASA Astronaut Jessica Meir
summary: NASA astronaut Jessica Meir was featured as a finalist in the 2025 NASA Photographer of the Year. She is set to launch on NASA’s SpaceX Crew-12 mission to the International Space Station in February 2026.

title: NASA’s SpaceX 34th Commercial Resupply Mission Overview
summary: NASA and SpaceX are targeting a mid-May launch for the 34th Commercial Resupply Mission to the International Space Station. The Dragon spacecraft will carry 6,500 pounds of supplies aboard a Falcon 9 rocket from Cape Canaveral.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: SpaceX and Chinese missions are contributing to a busy launch schedule with nine launches planned across the United States and China during the week of May 11. The launches span multiple activities in the space industry.

title: Full Stack Flight 12 completes WDR
summary: Full Stack Flight 12 completed a Wet Dress Rehearsal (WDR) after Booster 19's successful static fire test. The event was reported by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station while advancing its commercial sector towards reusability. The country is also working on deep space missions.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is undergoing conversion work, with Flight 12 approaching. The changes come over six months after the conclusion of Flight 11.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 flight, potentially marking a quiet period before an upcoming surge in activity.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, carrying CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its 12th Starship flight test with a revised trajectory. The company has issued notices for the upcoming mission, indicating a potential return to flight operations.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully launched its new Soyuz-5 rocket on its maiden flight from the Baikonur Cosmodrome. The debut marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A NASA astronaut and doctor discusses their upcoming mission to the International Space Station (ISS). The astronaut has experience in mass casualty events and military service overseas.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant return for the heavy-lift vehicle.

summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned, but no specific news or details are provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) has released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space missions.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: No specific news content was provided for analysis.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical parachute testing process for a space mission, involving a high-temperature bake-out to ensure reliability. This procedure is essential for validating the parachute system before an upcoming launch.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter diameter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to avoid microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for space applications, enabling secure handling of objects in microgravity. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, confirming its readiness for launch. The spacecraft is scheduled to lift off in early 2027 to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, which includes an online preparatory session in April-May and an in-person event at ESA Headquarters in Paris on June 8-9, 2026. The forum aims to gather feedback on topics affecting small and medium-sized enterprises in the space sector.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received, marking a significant milestone for the project. The exact location for the signal reception was not specified in the provided text.

title: Star Catcher Closes $65M Series A
summary: Star Catcher has secured $65 million in a Series A funding round led by B Capital, with Shield Capital and Cerberus Ventures co-leading the investment.

title: Fenix Space Flies Tow-Launch Prototype
summary: Fenix Space has successfully flown a tow-launch prototype, marking a significant milestone in their development. This achievement brings them closer to their goal of commercial spaceflight.

title: Aetherflux Rebrands, Pivots Business—and Raises $275M
summary: Aetherflux has rebranded to Cowboy Space Corporation as part of a business pivot and secured $275 million in funding to support its new direction.

title: SOCOM Taps SkyFi to Build Tactical EO Imagery Tools
summary: SOCOM has selected SkyFi to develop a prototype for a sovereign intelligence platform providing troops with faster access to geospatial imagery. The tool aims to enhance tactical decision-making using electro-optical imagery.

title: Lunar Outpost Closes $30M Series B, Unveils New Rover
summary: Lunar Outpost raised $30 million in a Series B funding round and unveiled a new rover. The investment reflects investor interest in supporting NASA's plans for a US lunar presence.

title: Rocket Lab To Acquire Motiv Space Systems
summary: Rocket Lab will acquire space robotics company Motiv Space Systems, rebranding it as Rocket Lab Robotics upon closure in Q2. The acquisition was announced alongside Rocket Lab's Q1 earnings.

title: HawkEye 360 Raises $416M in IPO
summary: HawkEye 360, a Virginia-based company, raised $416 million in its IPO by pricing 16 million shares at $26 each. The company had previously filed an S-1 indicating a price range of $24-$26.

title: SatVu Positions Thermal Imaging as the Missing EO Layer
summary: SatVu has resumed operations, positioning its thermal imaging satellites as a crucial missing layer in Earth observation (EO). The company aims to enhance EO capabilities with its advanced thermal imaging technology.

title: South Korea Pushes to Commercialize Quantum Research
summary: South Korea is accelerating efforts to commercialize quantum research, aiming to establish itself as a key player in the emerging quantum technology sector.

title: Overview Energy Wins Air Force Contract
summary: Overview Energy has secured an Air Force contract to provide continuous power to military bases using space-based solar energy, addressing vulnerabilities in fuel supply chains.

title: Space42 Enhances D2D Prospects with Skylo Partnership
summary: Space42 partnered with Skylo Technologies to enhance direct-to-device (D2D) connectivity using the Thuraya-4 geostationary satellite. This collaboration aims to deliver standards-based D2D services in the space industry.

title: Vitesse Systems and Accelint Become Part of New Defense Tech Company Lyntris
summary: Vitesse Systems and Accelint have merged to form a new defense technology company named Lyntris, specializing in connected battlefield solutions. Both companies were previously owned by Trive Capital.

title: Hughes Posts Decline in Broadband Subscribers and Service Revenue in Q1 2026
summary: Hughes Network Systems reported a decline in broadband subscribers and service revenue for Q1 2026, attributing the drop to competition from rival satellite and terrestrial technologies.

title: Aetherflux Rebrands as Cowboy Space, Expanding Plans to In-Orbit Compute and Launch
summary: Aetherflux, founded by former Robinhood co-founder Baiju Bhatt, has rebranded as Cowboy Space and is expanding its focus to in-orbit compute and launch infrastructure.

title: Greek Government Taps Planet for New Satellite Data Deal
summary: The Greek government has signed a 2-year, 7-figure contract with Planet for satellite data services. Planet Labs Germany, a subsidiary of Planet, secured this significant deal.

title: Operational AI is Hitting the Limits of Earth Observation Data
summary: AI in Earth Observation is transitioning from experimental use on curated datasets to operational deployment, where models must maintain performance across diverse and real-world conditions. This shift highlights the growing challenges and limitations of current Earth observation data for sustained AI applications.

title: Globalstar Grows Revenue 17% in Q1 Amid Amazon Acquisition Process
summary: Globalstar reported a 17% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 2026 amid its ongoing acquisition process by Amazon. The satellite operator's financial performance reflects its strategic position in the industry.

title: Rocket Lab Q1 Takeaways: Neutron Momentum, SBI Deal and Robotics Acquisition
summary: Rocket Lab reported record quarterly revenue in Q1 2026 and highlighted progress on its Neutron launch vehicle, a robotics acquisition, and an SBI deal.

title: Skyroot Secures $60M in Funding, Becoming India’s First Space ‘Unicorn’
summary: Skyroot Aerospace secured $60 million in funding, becoming India's first space 'unicorn'. The Indian launch startup is a rising star in the country's space economy.

title: HawkEye 360 Raises $416M, Pricing at High End for IPO
summary: HawkEye 360 raised $416 million in its initial public offering (IPO), becoming the second space company to go public this year. The signals intelligence company began trading on the stock market.
